meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you ship functional, high-impact features like user onboarding. By mastering this flow, you demonstrate your ability to manage state, handle edge cases, and improve user retention—the exact skills hiring managers hunt for. Build it, document it, and turn your repo into a career catalyst.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank screen, the cursor blinking with the weight of a thousand 'what-ifs.' Every job board asks for experience you don't have, and your GitHub is a quiet graveyard of half-finished tutorials. The industry doesn't care about your degree; it cares about your ability to solve the friction points that keep real businesses awake at night.

User onboarding is the ultimate proving ground for a developer. It’s where design meets data, where logic meets empathy, and where a user decides if your app is a tool or a toy. By building a sophisticated onboarding flow, you aren't just writing code—you are crafting the first impression of a product. It is time to transform your 'no portfolio' anxiety into a concrete, shippable asset.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the minimum viable steps from sign-up to the 'Aha!' moment. This proves you think like a product engineer, not just a coder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a 3-step user onboarding flow including fields for user profile preferences and a 'completed_onboarding' boolean flag.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a user-onboarding-schema.json file based on the provided logic.'

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Onboarding requires tracking progress. We will use a state machine approach to ensure the user can navigate back and forth without losing context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React hook called useOnboarding that manages the current step index and persists state to localStorage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts with persistent state logic.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive UI
steps.description: Create a modular UI component that renders different steps based on the current state. This demonstrates your ability to build reusable component architectures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Tailwind-styled OnboardingCard component that accepts a currentStep prop and renders content dynamically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build src/components/OnboardingCard.tsx using Tailwind CSS for a professional look.'

steps.title: Integrate Progress Analytics
steps.description: Adding a progress bar or tracking completion rate shows that you value user metrics—a high-level skill that separates seniors from juniors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that calculates percentage completion and triggers a console log when the user finishes onboarding.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a progress tracking utility to src/utils/analytics.ts.'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Your portfolio is only as good as your README. Document the 'why' behind your onboarding flow to show potential employers your thought process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md file template for this project, emphasizing the technical challenges solved in the user onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-quality README.md based on the current project structure.'

pro_tips: Always focus on 'empty state' design; how the app looks before the user does anything is often more important than the final dashboard.
Use Zod for schema validation in your onboarding forms to prevent bad data from hitting your database.
Implement 'optimistic UI' updates so the onboarding feels instantaneous, even if the backend is processing data.
Document your failures in the README; describing a bug you fixed is more impressive than a perfect project.
Keep your onboarding flow under 60 seconds; if it takes longer, you are asking for too much information too soon.
content.next_steps: Once you have finished the onboarding flow, deploy the project to Vercel and add a link to it in your LinkedIn 'Featured' section. Then, challenge yourself to add an 'Email Verification' step to the flow to practice handling asynchronous side effects.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade onboarding flow, a clean codebase, and a project that proves you can build real-world software. Don't let this momentum stall—your portfolio is waiting. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
